headline: AI Weekly: Micron boosts chip stocks, firms mull AI-related cuts
summary:
STORY: From chip stocks getting a fresh boost... to where chip workers have become among the most desirable partners.. This is AI Weekly.

:: AI Weekly

Chip stocks rebounded after bullish outlooks from U.S. firms Micron Technology and Qualcomm.

Micron said customers had committed to spend $22 billion on its chips.

It also forecast quarterly profit and revenue well above expectations, sending its shares 12% higher in after-hours trading.

Landsberg Bennett Chief Investment Officer Michael Landsberg says the AI boom has been transformational for memory chip makers like Micron:

“What’s happened in this space has been, typically this used to be more of a commodity, and so what’s happened now with AI, a lot of these chips are needed to make AI work faster and better so the demand has really been strong across the entire sector.”

Donald Trump has said Apple has agreed to work with Intel to design and manufacture its chips in the U.S.

The move would be a major boost for the American chipmaker's efforts to turn around its business.

A contract with Apple would lock in steady demand from the iPhone maker.

That would boost Intel's reputation and sales as it looks to close the gap with rival TSMC.

Oracle's total workforce declined 13%, or about 21,000 employees, in its fiscal 2026.

It's as the cloud computing giant continued restructuring its business... partly driven by the adoption of AI across its operations.

Up to 3,000 Santander employees in Spain could also be offered early retirement.

That's as the bank braces for impacts brought on by AI, Spanish newspaper Expansion reported on Wednesday.

SK Hynix overtook Samsung Electronics to become South Korea's most valuable listed company.

Shares in what is now the world's most valuable memory chipmaker rose to bring the company's market capitalization to $1.35 trillion.  

Employees at chip factories are also now part of South Korea’s A-list. 

Some workers at Samsung’s chip division are set for bonuses of about $416,000.

Around 14 times South Korea’s average annual salary.

Chip workers now rival doctors and lawyers as the nation’s most desirable partners.

Lee Sung-mi from matchmaking agency Sunoo says tech staffers now rate very highly:

"Scores for Samsung Electronics employees have recently risen from 83 to 87. As for clients who work at SK Hynix, their scores are expected to climb from around 80 to 83—showing just how highly favored the company has become."

content.title: AI Weekly: Micron boosts chip stocks, firms mull AI-related cuts

It also forecast quarterly profit and revenue well above expectations, sending its shares 12% higher in after-hours trading.

Landsberg Bennett Chief Investment Officer Michael Landsberg says the AI boom has been transformational for memory chip makers like Micron:

“What’s happened in this space has been, typically this used to be more of a commodity, and so what’s happened now with AI, a lot of these chips are needed to make AI work faster and better so the demand has really been strong across the entire sector.”

Donald Trump has said Apple has agreed to work with Intel to design and manufacture its chips in the U.S.

The move would be a major boost for the American chipmaker's efforts to turn around its business.

A contract with Apple would lock in steady demand from the iPhone maker.

That would boost Intel's reputation and sales as it looks to close the gap with rival TSMC.

Oracle's total workforce declined 13%, or about 21,000 employees, in its fiscal 2026.

It's as the cloud computing giant continued restructuring its business... partly driven by the adoption of AI across its operations.

Up to 3,000 Santander employees in Spain could also be offered early retirement.

That's as the bank braces for impacts brought on by AI, Spanish newspaper Expansion reported on Wednesday.

SK Hynix overtook Samsung Electronics to become South Korea's most valuable listed company.

Shares in what is now the world's most valuable memory chipmaker rose to bring the company's market capitalization to $1.35 trillion.  

Employees at chip factories are also now part of South Korea’s A-list. 

Some workers at Samsung’s chip division are set for bonuses of about $416,000.

Around 14 times South Korea’s average annual salary.

Chip workers now rival doctors and lawyers as the nation’s most desirable partners.

Lee Sung-mi from matchmaking agency Sunoo says tech staffers now rate very highly:

"Scores for Samsung Electronics employees have recently risen from 83 to 87. As for clients who work at SK Hynix, their scores are expected to climb from around 80 to 83—showing just how highly favored the company has become."

headline: Jumio Is the First Identity Intelligence Provider to Offer Global Digital ID Acceptance at Scale
summary: SUNNYVALE, Calif., June 25, 2026--Jumio, the leading provider of AI-powered identity intelligence, today announced that it has significantly expanded support for digital IDs across the globe, allowing Jumio customers to accept digital IDs in more than 60 countries and territories when onboarding new users, helping customers drive conversions and meet compliance mandates.

content.title: Jumio Is the First Identity Intelligence Provider to Offer Global Digital ID Acceptance at Scale

headline: Apple raises prices of MacBooks, iPads as memory costs skyrocket
summary: Apple  raised iPad and MacBook prices on Thursday, saying it could no longer shield customers from soaring memory and storage chip costs driven by the AI industry's datacenter buildout.  The move does not affect Apple's main cash cow, the iPhone. ‌But it would take starting price of the Neo - its lowest priced laptop aimed at winning marketshare from affordable Windows and Chromebook laptops - from $599 to $699 months ‌after launch.  The increase shows even the world's most valuable consumer electronics company with supply chain relationships that are the envy of the industry is not immune to a memory price surge that has dulled the ​outlook for smartphone and PC sales.

content.title: Apple raises prices of MacBooks, iPads as memory costs skyrocket
content.summary: Apple  raised iPad and MacBook prices on Thursday, saying it could no longer shield customers from soaring memory and storage chip costs driven by the AI industry's datacenter buildout.  The move does not affect Apple's main cash cow, the iPhone. ‌But it would take starting price of the Neo - its lowest priced laptop aimed at winning marketshare from affordable Windows and Chromebook laptops - from $599 to $699 months ‌after launch.  The increase shows even the world's most valuable consumer electronics company with supply chain relationships that are the envy of the industry is not immune to a memory price surge that has dulled the ​outlook for smartphone and PC sales.

headline: The 4th China International Supply Chain Expo: Digital Tech Chain Links Global Upstream and Downstream in the Intelligent Economy
summary: The Digital Technology Chain exhibition area at the Fourth China International Supply Chain Expo (CISCE) features 58 booths and brings together 228 upstream and downstream companies from 11 countries across the global industrial chain. This year, the section has been upgraded from "Digital Technology Chain" to "Intelligent Digital Technology Chain," with the debut of a dedicated artificial intelligence (AI) Zone, reflecting the growing role of AI across industrial value chains.

content.title: The 4th China International Supply Chain Expo: Digital Tech Chain Links Global Upstream and Downstream in the Intelligent Economy
content.summary: The Digital Technology Chain exhibition area at the Fourth China International Supply Chain Expo (CISCE) features 58 booths and brings together 228 upstream and downstream companies from 11 countries across the global industrial chain. This year, the section has been upgraded from "Digital Technology Chain" to "Intelligent Digital Technology Chain," with the debut of a dedicated artificial intelligence (AI) Zone, reflecting the growing role of AI across industrial value chains.

headline: Why the Dow Is Soaring But the Nasdaq Is Falling
summary: The stock market is leaving Big Tech stocks in the dust. The Dow surged 733 points, or 1.4%, while the S&P 500 gained 0.3%. The Nasdaq, which was up nearly 1% at the open, was down 0.6%. More than four-in-five S&P 500 stocks were set to close higher, but the Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF dropped 2%.

headline: Wall Street drifts in mixed trading after Micron soars and Apple drops
summary: The U.S. stock market is drifting in mixed trading Thursday after several artificial-intelligence stocks veered back up their roller-coaster ride, while Apple dropped after hiking prices on many of its products.  Micron Technology helped lead the market after jumping 14%.  The maker of computer memory reported much stronger profit and revenue for the latest quarter than analysts expected, and it gave a stronger growth forecast for the current quarter than Wall Street expected.

Micron Technology helped lead the market after jumping 14%.  The maker of computer memory reported much stronger profit and revenue for the latest quarter than analysts expected, and it gave a stronger growth forecast for the current quarter than Wall Street expected.

headline: Apple raises prices for MacBooks and iPads, as costs soar over AI
summary: Apple said Thursday it was raising prices for its MacBook computers, iPad tablets and other products, citing spiraling memory and storage costs sparked by the rise of artificial intelligence.The price hikes -- the first concrete change stemming from outgoing CEO Tim Cook's repeated warnings about rising costs -- sent Apple shares plummeting more than 4.7 percent in morning trade.

headline: The Dow May Finally Close Above 52,000. Fourth Time's the Charm?
summary: The Dow is on track to close above 52,000 for the first time.  The blue-chip index was up 214 points, or 0.4%, to 52,060.  A close above 52,000 would mark its fourth thousand-point milestone of 2026; the Dow closed above 51,000 18 trading days ago, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

A close above 52,000 would mark its fourth thousand-point milestone of 2026; the Dow closed above 51,000 18 trading days ago, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

headline: May PCE: Inflation ticks up to highest level since 2023
summary: May's Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index — the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge — saw inflation rise 0.4% month-over-month and 4.1% year-over-year Core PCE, which excludes food and energy prices, rose 0.3% monthly and 3.4% annually, marking the highest level since 2023. Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman is joined by Yahoo Finance Tech Editor Dan Howley and Epistrophy Capital Research chief market strategist Cory Johnson to further examine rising price trends.

content.title: May PCE: Inflation ticks up to highest level since 2023
content.description:
May's Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index — the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge — saw inflation rise 0.4% month-over-month and 4.1% year-over-year

Core PCE, which excludes food and energy prices, rose 0.3% monthly and 3.4% annually, marking the highest level since 2023.

Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman is joined by Yahoo Finance Tech Editor Dan Howley and Epistrophy Capital Research chief market strategist Cory Johnson to further examine rising price trends.
content.summary: May's Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index — the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge — saw inflation rise 0.4% month-over-month and 4.1% year-over-year Core PCE, which excludes food and energy prices, rose 0.3% monthly and 3.4% annually, marking the highest level since 2023. Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman is joined by Yahoo Finance Tech Editor Dan Howley and Epistrophy Capital Research chief market strategist Cory Johnson to further examine rising price trends.

headline: Nasdaq falls again on mixed day for US stocks, oil prices rise
summary: The Nasdaq fell for the fourth straight session Thursday behind weakness in Apple and other tech giants while oil prices pushed higher after a cargo ship was attacked in the Strait of Hormuz. The cargo ship was damaged by an unknown projectile off the Omani coast in the strategic strait on Thursday, prompting the International Maritime Organization to halt an evacuation of crews trapped by the US-Iran war.

The prices changes, all at least $100, are laid out on  Apple’s  website.

headline: Mag 7 Stocks Shed Nearly $3 Trillion This Month
summary: A massive rotation at the expense of big AI spenders has the Magnificent Seven on pace to shed $3 trillion this month.  Collectively, Tesla, Apple, Microsoft, Meta Platforms, Nvidia, Amazon.com, and Alphabet have lost $2.99 trillion in market cap so far in June, according to Dow Jones Market Data.  The Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF is on pace for its worst month on record.

content.title: Mag 7 Stocks Shed Nearly $3 Trillion This Month
content.summary: A massive rotation at the expense of big AI spenders has the Magnificent Seven on pace to shed $3 trillion this month.  Collectively, Tesla, Apple, Microsoft, Meta Platforms, Nvidia, Amazon.com, and Alphabet have lost $2.99 trillion in market cap so far in June, according to Dow Jones Market Data.  The Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF is on pace for its worst month on record.

headline: Wall Street drifts to a mixed finish after Micron soars and Apple drops
summary: The U.S. stock market meandered to a mixed finish Thursday after several artificial-intelligence stocks  veered back up their roller-coaster ride, while Apple dropped after hiking prices on many of its products.  The Dow Jones Industrial Average added 71 points, or 0.1%, and the Nasdaq composite fell 0.5%.  Micron Technology helped lead the market after jumping 15.7%.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average added 71 points, or 0.1%, and the Nasdaq composite fell 0.5%.  Micron Technology helped lead the market after jumping 15.7%.
